# Unofficial Meeting Notes
## Conroe Independent School District Board of Trustees — Regular Meeting
### March 20, 2018

> **Note:** These are **unofficial notes** based on an auto-generated transcript of the meeting. They may contain errors or omissions. Please consult official board minutes for the authoritative record.

---

## Call to Order & Opening
- Meeting called to order at **6:00 PM**; quorum confirmed.
- **Invocation:** Board member Mr. Hubert
- **Presentation of Colors:** The Woodlands College Park Marine Corps JROTC Color Guard, led by Major Cody Stewart and Sergeant Major Chris Wounds
- **Pledges led by:** Mr. Sanders (Pledge of Allegiance); Texas Pledge also recited

---

## Special Board Recognitions (Items 2A–2E)

### The Woodlands High School Swimming & Diving Teams
**Presenter:** Principal Greg Coulson and Head Coach Jeremy Wade

Key accomplishments recognized:
- **Girls team:** Back-to-back **State Champions**
- **Boys team:** **State Runner-Up**
- Both boys and girls won **District and Regional titles**
- **5 school records broken; 1 state record set**
- **Boys State Champion 200 Medley Relay Team:** Alberto Gomez (Sr.), Keenan Wolf (Sr.), Tyler Hewlett (Fr.), Luke George (So.)
  - Alberto Gomez broke two school records, including a 19-year-old 100 backstroke record; committed to Texas A&M
- **Girls State Champion 200 Medley Relay** (new state record): Lucy Nordmann, Kaitlyn Rhaneyra, Lily Nordmann, Valerie Anstaphil
- **Girls State Champion 400 Free Relay:** Lucy Nordmann, Ainsley Everett, Madelyn Krypton, Sid Roycraft, Lily Nordmann
- **Lucy Nordmann:** Individual state champion in 200 Freestyle and 100 Backstroke (won 100 backstroke four years in a row); committed to Stanford
- Multiple other swimmers recognized for individual placements at state
- Coach Wade noted many swimmers achieved All-American time standards and will receive Academic All-State/All-American accolades

Board President Bush congratulated the swimmers and coaches, noting the extraordinary commitment required of competitive swimmers.

---

## Citizen Participation (Item 2F)

### Heather Carrier — Parent (Oak Ridge Elementary & Irons Junior High)
Ms. Carrier expressed serious concerns about **school safety and security** in the wake of recent school shootings:
- Reported that her 7th-grade son at Irons Junior High learned of a student who allegedly threatened to bring a gun to school; confirmed this involved **two separate incidents with two different students** just days apart
- Described receiving "scripted responses" from school administrators that did not alleviate her concerns; praised Principal Fuller for a more personal, extended conversation
- Noted that security measures identical to those in place (armed officers, hallway patrols) did not prevent the Parkland, FL shooting (Feb. 14, 2018) or the Great Mills, MD shooting (that same morning, March 20)
- Cited statistics: 208 school shootings since Columbine (1999), with 141 people killed; 73% of shooters had no prior criminal record
- **Urged the board not to arm teachers**, stating teachers should not bear the burden of deciding to take a child's life
- Drew an analogy to airport security evolution after 9/11
- Asked the board: **"How much exactly is my child's life worth?"**
- Requested more formidable, comprehensive security measures in schools

---

## Naming of Principal — York Junior High School (Item 6A — Moved Up)

**Recommendation:** Dr. Stockton recommended **Bryan Lee** as principal of York Junior High School (part of the new Grand Oaks feeder area).

**Vote: Approved unanimously** (by voice vote)

Mr. Lee thanked the board, acknowledged his family (wife Nikki, daughter Lainey, son Camden, and other family members present), and expressed his commitment to serving the Grand Oaks community and continuing the work started by Dr. Povich.

---

## Closed Session
- Board entered closed session at **6:28 PM** as authorized by Section 551.074 of the Texas Open Meetings Act
- Board returned to open session at **6:44 PM**

---

## Naming of Lone Finalist for Superintendent of Schools (Item 3)

**Motion by Board President Bush:** To select **Dr. Curtis Null** as the **lone finalist for Superintendent of Conroe ISD**.

**Seconded** and followed by extensive board discussion.

### Board Member Statements:

- **Mr. Hubert:** Praised Dr. Null's ability to communicate and advocate for students, administrators, and teachers; noted his leadership is evidenced by the number of people supporting him
- **Mr. Kidd (Scott Kidd):** Served nearly 8 years on the board; said Dr. Null "nailed it" in the interview; highlighted his student-centric approach and outstanding communication skills; noted importance of continuity from Dr. Stockton's leadership
- **Another board member:** Called the decision to recommend Dr. Null one of the easiest decisions in six years on the board; expressed absolute confidence in his ability; humorously noted Dr. Null's inability to recite the "power pledge" on the spot during the interview
- **Another board member:** Shared personal connection — first met Dr. Null's father as an educator years ago; praised the family legacy of genuine love for students
- **Board President Bush:** Reflected on years of conversations with Dr. Stockton about personnel development; praised both Dr. Stockton's vision in grooming future leaders and Dr. Null's character; made personal remarks about Dr. Null's role in supporting her family
- **Another board member:** Described getting to know Dr. Null personally and professionally; noted former students and mentees still quote his teachings; expressed confidence he will be "the next greatest superintendent in the state of Texas"

### Tribute to Dr. Don Stockton (Outgoing Superintendent):
Multiple board members praised Dr. Stockton's 15-year tenure, noting he:
- Transformed CISD from a little-known district mired in controversy to one of the most recognized in the state
- Demonstrated unmatched integrity and work ethic
- Served as mentor to Dr. Null and many other district leaders

**Vote: Approved unanimously** (by voice vote)

> **Note:** Per Texas law, the lone finalist designation begins a mandatory 21-day waiting period before the board can take final action to hire.

### Dr. Curtis Null's Remarks:
Dr. Null delivered an extended, emotional address:
- Expressed honor at assuming the role in the district where he began his teaching career
- Praised Dr. Stockton's 15-year tenure and personal mentorship
- Acknowledged that CISD's willingness to hire an internal candidate reflects confidence in the entire district team
- Credited the district's educators for his professional development
- Introduced family members: parents Mike and Lynda Null, wife Tania (a Pre-K teacher in CISD), children Travis and Kaylie (students at Conroe High School), in-laws, and childhood best friend Leonard Guitars and family
- Emphasized CISD's culture of love — for students, for colleagues, and for education — citing examples including community support after Hurricane Harvey
- Challenged everyone present to thank someone who made a difference in their lives
- Stated: "Every day in education is special... this job is great because it matters so much"

Board President Bush congratulated Dr. Null and told him: "You only need to fill one set of shoes... and those are yours."

**Brief recess taken** for attendees to congratulate Dr. Null.

---

## Consent Agenda (Item 5)

No items were pulled for separate discussion.

**Vote: Approved unanimously** (by voice vote)

---

## Action/Discussion Items

### Item 7A: 2018–2019 TEA Homeless Children and Youth Grant
**Presenter:** Dr. Hines

- Requesting board approval to **apply** for a competitive grant for homeless children and youth
- Grant amount: up to **$35,625** for up to **3 years**
- Would fund **42% of one new position** (homeless liaison); remaining 42% funded locally; 16% from existing Title I funds
- Current homeless student count: approximately **945 students** (up significantly due to Hurricane Harvey)
  - ~21 displaced from other storms; ~665+ displaced due to Harvey
  - Pre-Harvey baseline: approximately 475 students
- Position would support connecting homeless students with services, arranging transportation, assisting with registration, etc.
- Current liaison Linda Gowen wears multiple hats
- Board questions addressed: "Homeless" has a broad federal definition including families in transitional housing or doubled-up with other families; Title I connection does not preclude serving students at non-Title I schools (all homeless students qualify for Title I under McKinney-Vento Act)
- Board members expressed concern about the growing homeless student population in the district and Montgomery County

**Vote: Approved unanimously** (by voice vote)

---

### Item 8A: Clark Intermediate School Mascot and Colors (Informational)
**Presenter:** Dr. Phelps

- Katherine Johnson Clark Intermediate School scheduled to open in the future
- Survey of approximately 300 future Clark students conducted
- **Proposed mascot:** Clark **Cubs** (orange and blue colors)
  - Colors align with the Grand Oaks feeder pattern
  - Thematic connection: Bradley Bears → Clark Cubs → Grand Oaks Grizzlies
  - Orange is also a favorite color of the school's namesake, Kathy Clark
- **Informational only** — will return for formal board vote at the next meeting

---

### Item 8B: Guaranteed Maximum Price — District-Wide Safety & Security Upgrades Phase 3
**Presenter:** Mr. Foster

- **Phase 3 (final phase)** of the district-wide safety and security upgrades project from the 2015 bond referendum
- Construction manager at risk: **Elsor Constructors** (selected March 22, 2016)
- **GMP: $3,715,314**
- Upon completion, every campus in the district will have been touched by security upgrades
- Upgrades include:
  - Secure vestibule entries at elementary and intermediate schools (controlled access)
  - Access control on exterior doors
  - Camera system upgrades
  - Multi-layered security approach

**Vote: Approved unanimously** (by voice vote)

---

| **Stadium Scoreboards** | Design/fabrication complete with Daktronics; installation begins after soccer season ends |
| **Flex 19** | Working through MUD district jurisdictions on drainage; mobilizing on site |
| **Austin Elementary Renovation** | Site preparation underway; reorganizing traffic flow for safety |
| **Irons Junior High Addition** | 10 classrooms being added; good progress over spring break; scheduled for use by winter break 2018 |
| **New Junior High School** | Nearing end of City of Conroe permitting; mobilization imminent |

### Item 9A: Award of RFP 17-01-01A — Contracted Educational Services
**Presenter:** Mr. Ice

- Award to **21 vendors** for professional development and educational consulting services
- Estimated annual expenditure: approximately **$12,000**
- 223 of estimated 350 vendors awarded since June 2017
- Contracts firm through March 31, 2019, with option to renew annually through March 31, 2023

**Vote: Approved unanimously** (by voice vote)

---

### Item 9B: Transfer of General Fund Unassigned Fund Balance to Capital Projects Fund
**Presenter:** Mr. Ice

- Recommended transfer of **$8.2 million** from general fund unassigned fund balance to capital projects fund
  - ~$5 million for Irons Junior High classroom additions
  - ~$3.2 million for scoreboard replacements at Moorehead Stadium, Woodforest Bank Stadium, and the Natatorium
- Noted that scoreboard funding will be recouped through advertising sales over several years

**Vote: Approved unanimously** (by voice vote)

- Community utilization was already evident within the first few days of opening
